Hyundai Creta.mileage: 1.5L MPi Petrol MT: 17.4 kmpl, 1.5L MPi Petrol IVT: 17.7 kmpl, 1.5L U2 CRDi Diesel MT: 21.8 kmpl, 1.5L U2 CRDi Diesel AT: 19.1 kmpl, 1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol DCT: 18.4 kmpl
Hyundai Creta.driveability: tweaked the final drive ratios of the 6-speed manual gearbox for better driveability inside the city
Hyundai Creta.torque: 1.5L MPi Petrol: 144 Nm @ 4500 rpm, 1.5L U2 CRDi Diesel: 250 Nm @ 1500-2750 rpm, 1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol: 253 Nm @ 1500-3500 rpm
Hyundai Creta.response: fast throttle response in Dynamic mode
Hyundai Creta.off_road: Ground Clearance: 190 mm, Drive Type: FWD. Approach/Departure Angle: Not found
Hyundai Creta.manual_transmission_performance: Smooth and precise, with a light clutch, making it easy to drive in city traffic.
Hyundai Creta.automatic_transmission_performance: CVT (1.5L NA Petrol): smooth and responsive, avoids the rubber-band effect well. DCT (1.5L Turbo-Petrol): quick and smooth, offers brisk acceleration, paddle shifters available. 6-speed AT (1.5L Diesel): well-tuned, offers smooth shifts and good kickdown response.
Hyundai Creta.pedal_operation: light, if a little springy step to the clutch
Hyundai Creta.pedal_travel: clutch pedal travel is too long
Hyundai Creta.stiff_on_pot_holes: Comfortable ride, soaking up most bumps and potholes with ease. Suspension is well-tuned for Indian roads, providing a good balance between comfort and stability.
Hyundai Creta.bumps: excellent suspension setup, absorbing bumps and potholes effortlessly
Hyundai Creta.shocks: The new suspension tune means it handles better than ever and leans predictably.
Hyundai Creta.nvh: Improvements in the NVH package, leading to a very refined engine.
Hyundai Creta.powertrain_nvh: Some engine noise is present, but the drive is serene.
Hyundai Creta.wind_nvh: Wind insulation could be a bit better.
Hyundai Creta.road_nvh: Road noise shows itself over rough patches.
Hyundai Creta.wind_noise: Wind insulation could be a bit better.
Hyundai Creta.tire_noise: Tyre noise is about as much as anything else here.
Hyundai Creta.turbo_noise: No significant turbo whine is audible inside the cabin.
Hyundai Creta.jerks: A bit jerky while getting off the mark.
Hyundai Creta.steering: Hydraulic Power Steering, new steering wheel design featuring four dots
Hyundai Creta.manoeuvring: Bigger dimensions make city manoeuvring and parking harder
Hyundai Creta.straight_ahead_stability: Zero drama in a straight line (2020 model)
Hyundai Creta.brakes: Front: Disc, Rear: Disc, ABS, EBD
Hyundai Creta.vehicle_safety_features: ABS, EBD, ESC, VSM, HAC, ISOFIX
Hyundai Creta.adas: Lane Departure Warning, Blind-Spot Collision Warning & Avoidance Assist, Forward Collision Warning & Avoidance Assist, Smart Cruise Control with Stop & Go, Lane Keeping Assist, Lane Following Assist, Driver Attention Warning, High Beam Assist, Leading Vehicle Departure Alert, Safe Exit Warning
Hyundai Creta.seats_restraint: 3-point seatbelts for all seats, Seat Belt Reminder (SBR) for driver and passenger
Hyundai Creta.interior: All-black interior with distinctive upholstery, N Line-spec steering wheel and gear stick (for N Line variant)
Hyundai Creta.climate_control: Automatic single-zone climate control; Rear AC Vents: Yes
Hyundai Creta.seats: The front seats are well-cushioned and offer good support, making them comfortable for long drives. The rear seats are also comfortable, with good thigh support and adequate backrest recline. The middle passenger in the rear might find it a bit cramped due to the transmission tunnel and AC vents.
Hyundai Creta.seat_cushion: Front seats are well-cushioned and offer good support. Rear seats have good thigh support.
Hyundai Creta.seat_features_detailed: 8-Way Power Adjustable Driver Seat, Ventilated Front Seats
Hyundai Creta.rear_seat_features: Foldable Rear Seat, Rear Seat Center Arm Rest, Rear Cup Holders
Hyundai Creta.visibility: Visibility from the driver's seat is generally good, thanks to the large windows and well-positioned ORVMs. However, the thick C-pillars can create a blind spot when reversing or changing lanes.
Hyundai Creta.soft_trims: The dashboard features a mix of soft-touch materials and hard plastics, with soft-touch materials on the upper part of the dash and door pads.
Hyundai Creta.armrest: Front Centre Armrest (well-positioned, good support), Rear Centre Armrest with cup holders.
Hyundai Creta.egress: Entry and exit are relatively easy, thanks to the wide-opening doors and appropriate seat height. The sill height is not too high.
Hyundai Creta.ingress: Entry and exit are relatively easy, thanks to the wide-opening doors and appropriate seat height.
Hyundai Creta.seatbelt_features: Front Seatbelt Pretensioner, Force Limiter Seatbelts. Details on rear seatbelt features and height adjusters not specified.
Hyundai Creta.apple_carplay: Wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto
Hyundai Creta.button: Steering Wheel Controls, Start Engine Button
Hyundai Creta.cruise_control: Smart Cruise Control with Stop & Go
Hyundai Creta.parking_camera: 360-degree Surround View Monitor (SVM) with 2D & 3D view, Rear Parking Camera with Display on Infotainment
Hyundai Creta.orvm: electrically adjustable, auto-fold, integrated turn indicator
Hyundai Creta.parking: Front and rear parking sensors, parking brake with auto hold, hill assist
Hyundai Creta.chasis: Integrated chassis and body designed for strength and safety
Hyundai Creta.engine_displacement: 1.5L MPi Petrol: 1497 cc, 1.5L U2 CRDi Diesel: 1493 cc, 1.5L Turbo GDi Petrol: 1482 cc

Mahindra Thar.mileage: close to 10 kmpl in the city and 14 kmpl on the highway (for 2023 Thar 2WD)
Mahindra Thar.performance_feel: amazing as a driving experience, improved from the touch, feel and experience point of view
Mahindra Thar.driveability: Fun-to-drive SUV, easy to drive in the city and comfortable on the highway. Diesel engine is tractable and easy to drive in the city. Petrol engine is refined and offers good performance.
Mahindra Thar.response: various drive modes offer noticeable differences in throttle response, allowing drivers to tailor the car's behaviour to their preference (Thar Roxx)
Mahindra Thar.city_performance: Diesel engine has good low-end torque, making it easy to drive in the city. Petrol engine offers good performance in the city.
Mahindra Thar.highway_performance: Petrol engine offers better highway performance and is refined. Comfortable on the highway.
Mahindra Thar.off_road: Available with 4x4, known for legendary off-road DNA and capable of dispatching challenging off-road trails
Mahindra Thar.manual_transmission_performance: Manual gearbox is smooth to operate, and the clutch is light.
Mahindra Thar.automatic_transmission_performance: The torque converter automatic gearbox holds on to the gear for a bit longer than needed.
Mahindra Thar.pedal_operation: The clutch action is pretty hefty.
Mahindra Thar.ride: A bouncy ride was a serial complaint from co-passengers.
Mahindra Thar.stiff_on_pot_holes: Dismisses potholes and any tarmac blemishes with contempt.
Mahindra Thar.bumps: Suspension absorbs bumps and undulations well, but sharp potholes can be felt inside the cabin. Ride is a bit firm at low speeds, improves with speed.
Mahindra Thar.shocks: The Thar’s suspension setup is on the stiffer side, which helps with its off-road prowess but can make the ride a bit bouncy on broken city roads. Over undulations, it tends to rock side-to-side.
Mahindra Thar.nvh: The diesel engine is quite audible inside the cabin, especially at higher RPMs, and there's a noticeable amount of engine vibration that can be felt through the steering wheel and pedals. Road and wind noise are also quite prominent at highway speeds, making conversations a bit difficult.
Mahindra Thar.powertrain_nvh: the Thar is still noisier inside than your average SUV
Mahindra Thar.wind_nvh: wind noise but the Thar is still noisier inside than your average SUV
Mahindra Thar.road_nvh: Road noise is quite prominent at highway speeds.
Mahindra Thar.wind_noise: wind noise but the Thar is still noisier inside than your average SUV
Mahindra Thar.tire_noise: Tyre noise is noticeable, especially from the all-terrain tyres.
Mahindra Thar.turbo_noise: The turbocharger whine is audible, particularly under hard acceleration.
Mahindra Thar.jerks: The manual transmission can be a bit notchy, leading to some jerkiness if not timed correctly.
Mahindra Thar.shakes: a bit of body-on-chassis wobble at high speeds
Mahindra Thar.shudder: At very low speeds or when crawling in traffic, there can be a slight engine shudder, especially with the diesel manual.
Mahindra Thar.grabby: The brakes offer decent stopping power, but the pedal feel can be a bit grabby initially, requiring some getting used to for smooth modulation.
Mahindra Thar.spongy: Good progression, strong bite, inspires confidence.
Mahindra Thar.rattle: No noticeable rattles or squeaks even over rough terrain.
Mahindra Thar.manoeuvring: tight manoeuvring in crowded urban conditions a bit difficult
Mahindra Thar.stability: Equipped with stability control, hill-ascent control, and electronic stability program (ESP), providing good stability for long drives.
Mahindra Thar.corner_stability: Body roll is present but well-controlled and doesn't feel unnerving. Holds its line well through corners.
Mahindra Thar.straight_ahead_stability: Feels planted and stable on highways, even at higher speeds. Crosswinds can be felt but don't significantly affect stability.
Mahindra Thar.braking: Expected to feature rear-wheel disc brakes, in addition to existing front disc brakes.
Mahindra Thar.brakes: Front: Disc, Rear: Drum, ABS with EBD
Mahindra Thar.brake_performance: Good, with a strong bite and good progression. Inspires confidence even during hard braking.
Mahindra Thar.vehicle_safety_features: ABS with EBD, Electronic Stability Program (ESP) with Roll-over Mitigation, Hill Hold Control, Hill Descent Control, ISOFIX Child Seat Mounts
Mahindra Thar.impact: 5-star safety rating in Bharat NCAP for Thar Roxx
Mahindra Thar.seats_restraint: Features seat belts and seatbelt reminders for all seats, seatbelt pre-tensioners, and ISOFIX child seat anchor points.
Mahindra Thar.interior: Rugged and functional. Hard plastics are durable and well-finished. Fit-and-finish is good for a vehicle of its type. Overall design is practical, but no premium soft-touch materials.
Mahindra Thar.climate_control: Dual-zone climate control and rear AC vents
Mahindra Thar.seats: Seat with multipoint recline provides comfort
Mahindra Thar.seat_cushion: Front seats: comfortable with good cushioning and decent thigh support. Rear seats: a bit upright and lack adequate under-thigh support for longer journeys.
Mahindra Thar.seat_material: All-black leatherette upholstery with suede inserts; premium upholstery options, leatherette trims, and a choice of Mocha Brown interiors for 4x4
Mahindra Thar.visibility: Generally good due to upright seating and large windows. Thick A-pillars create a small manageable blind spot. Rear visibility is decent, though spare wheel on tailgate can obstruct view slightly.
Mahindra Thar.soft_trims: Soft-touch materials on the upper dashboard
Mahindra Thar.egress: High sill means lifting legs quite a bit. Getting out of rear seats requires effort due to two-door design.
Mahindra Thar.ingress: A bit of a climb due to high ground clearance and step-in height. Getting into rear seats requires effort due to two-door design.
Mahindra Thar.seatbelt_features: Front Seat Pretensioners & Force Limiter Seatbelts
Mahindra Thar.apple_carplay: Wired Apple CarPlay and Android Auto
Mahindra Thar.audio_system: Harman Kardon 9-speaker audio system with Dolby Atmos
Mahindra Thar.led: LED projector headlights with daytime running lights
Mahindra Thar.sunroof: None (available with Convertible Soft Top or Fixed Hard Top/Soft Top)
Mahindra Thar.orvm: Electrically Adjustable, Power Folding, Turn Signal On ORVM
Mahindra Thar.sensitivity: Steering is light at low speeds, gains weight at higher speeds. Good throttle response. Brakes offer decent stopping power, but pedal feel could be more progressive.
Mahindra Thar.wireless_carplay: Yes (Wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto)

summary:
**Price Positioning**
Focus on establishing a competitive entry point, potentially aligning with or slightly below Thar's ₹11.35 Lakh base to attract value-conscious buyers. Aim for a wide price band, but strategically avoid exceeding Creta's top-end of ₹20.20 Lakh unless offering substantially more premium features. Avoid overpricing initial variants, which could deter customers comparing against established rivals with robust offerings at similar entry levels.

**Engine & Performance**
Prioritize a balanced powertrain that offers smooth drivability for daily use, targeting torque figures comfortably above Creta's 144 Nm petrol to ensure responsive acceleration. While Creta's diesel offers 250 Nm, consider a slightly more spirited feel without sacrificing efficiency. Avoid chasing the Thar's extreme acceleration (6.3s 0-100 kph) or off-road torque, as this niche focus might compromise broader appeal and fuel economy.

**Fuel Efficiency**
Make best-in-class fuel efficiency a core product strength, aiming to significantly surpass both competitors. Target mileage figures beyond Creta's 17.4 kmpl (petrol) and 21.8 kmpl (diesel), as this is a major differentiator, especially against Thar's low 10-14 kmpl. Avoid any design or engineering choices that compromise this key metric for marginal gains in power or size, as efficiency drives purchase decisions.

**Safety Standards**
Develop a comprehensive safety package that at minimum matches Creta's ABS, EBD, ESC, VSM, HAC, and ISOFIX. Aim to exceed these by incorporating advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) or additional airbags to set a new benchmark. Avoid compromising on active and passive safety features, as both competitors offer strong foundations, and consumer expectations for safety are continually rising.

**NVH Levels**
Prioritize achieving superior Noise, Vibration, and Harshness (NVH) levels, aiming to replicate or surpass Creta's "improved" and "very refined engine" experience. A quiet and smooth cabin is crucial for premium perception and passenger comfort, especially on longer journeys. Avoid allowing significant engine noise (like Thar's audible diesel) or road/wind noise to penetrate the cabin, as this detracts from overall refinement.

**Ride & Handling**
Focus on delivering a "comfortable and enjoyable ride" that excels in urban and highway conditions, mirroring Creta's strength. Prioritize a well-tuned suspension setup that absorbs road imperfections effectively for passenger comfort and stability. Avoid a harsh or overly stiff ride that might appeal to a niche sporty segment but alienates the majority of customers seeking a compliant and comfortable daily driver.

**Interior & Features**
Offer a modern, premium, and feature-rich interior, including a large, high-definition infotainment screen (at least 10.25-inch) and a panoramic sunroof, key selling points for the Creta. Ensure ample boot space, aiming for equal or greater than Creta's 433 litres, and distinctive upholstery. Avoid a utilitarian or basic interior, a smaller infotainment display (like Thar's 7-inch), or omitting high-demand features like a sunroof.

**User Satisfaction**
Design the product to consistently achieve a high user rating, striving for at least 4.3/5, by excelling in overall comfort, features, reliability, and after-sales service. Leverage initial customer feedback to address any issues swiftly and proactively. Avoid overlooking the importance of a seamless ownership experience, as consistent high user satisfaction (seen in both Creta and Thar) builds long-term brand loyalty.

positives: The Creta offers a comprehensive list of segment-leading features like ventilated seats, a panoramic sunroof, and a large infotainment system.
The 1.5L turbo petrol and 1.5L diesel engines are known for their smoothness and strong performance, especially when paired with automatic transmissions.
The cabin boasts good quality materials and a well-finished design, giving it a premium feel for its segment.
The suspension is tuned for a comfortable ride, absorbing most road imperfections well, making it suitable for city and highway cruising.
The Creta's distinctive and modern exterior styling gives it a strong road presence and contributes to its premium appeal.
The rear seats offer ample legroom and shoulder room, making it comfortable for three passengers on shorter journeys.
negatives: The light steering and noticeable body roll limit the Creta's appeal for enthusiastic drivers, lacking precise feedback.
The fully loaded variants can become quite expensive, pushing it into a higher price bracket compared to some rivals.
The 1.5L naturally aspirated petrol engine offers only adequate performance and can be thirsty for its output.
The integrated touchscreen for climate control can be distracting to use while driving, as it lacks tactile feedback from physical buttons.
While bold, some design cues, particularly the split headlamps or rear light bar, might not appeal to everyone's taste.
Taller passengers might find the under-thigh support in the rear seats to be less than ideal on longer journeys due to seat angle.
Hyundai Creta.video_title: Search: Hyundai Creta review Autocar India

positives: The Creta consistently offers a highly competitive list of comfort, convenience, and safety features, often setting benchmarks in its segment.
Engine options, particularly with automatic transmissions, are typically praised for their smooth operation and refined power delivery, making city driving effortless.
The suspension setup is generally tuned for a comfortable ride, absorbing bumps well and making it suitable for daily commutes and urban conditions.
The cabin often feels premium with good material quality and a well-laid-out dashboard, contributing to an upmarket experience for the price point.
The distinctive design, while sometimes polarizing, gives the Creta a commanding and recognizable presence on the road.
negatives: The steering often feels numb and lacks the directness and feedback desired by enthusiasts, particularly during spirited driving.
While comfortable, the handling can be a bit floaty at higher speeds and through corners, making it less of a 'driver's car' compared to some rivals.
The exterior styling, especially the front fascia of certain generations, can be quite polarizing, not appealing to all tastes despite its distinctiveness.
The soft suspension setup, while good for comfort, can lead to noticeable body roll when cornering aggressively or during quick lane changes.
While feature-packed, the top-end variants command a significant premium, pushing into a price bracket where other, potentially larger, SUVs might be considered.

positives: The Thar offers unmatched off-road capability, easily tackling challenging terrains with its robust ladder-frame chassis and advanced 4x4 system.
It boasts an iconic, rugged design and significant road presence that turns heads and exudes a strong adventure lifestyle image.
Both the mStallion petrol and mHawk diesel engines provide strong performance and ample torque, making it enjoyable to drive on and off-road.
The interior is a significant upgrade from its predecessor, featuring a touchscreen infotainment system, improved ergonomics, and better quality materials.
Its commanding driving position provides excellent visibility and contributes to the SUV's robust and adventurous feel.
The Thar represents excellent value for money as a dedicated lifestyle off-roader, offering a unique experience at its price point.
negatives: The two-door configuration severely restricts rear seat access and offers limited comfort for adult passengers on longer journeys.
Boot space is minimal, making the Thar impractical for carrying significant luggage or for family trips.
The off-road focused suspension results in a firm and bouncy ride quality, especially over broken tarmac, impacting passenger comfort.
Noise, Vibration, and Harshness (NVH) levels are relatively high, with noticeable engine noise and road roar at higher speeds.
Its wide turning radius and substantial dimensions can make maneuvering and parking challenging in tight city environments.
Some interior ergonomics, such as the placement of power window switches on the center console, can be inconvenient for daily use.
Mahindra Thar.video_title: Search: Mahindra Thar review Autocar India

positives: The Mahindra Thar boasts an instantly recognizable, rugged design that commands significant road presence and makes a strong style statement.
Equipped with a capable 4x4 system, low-range gearbox, and generous ground clearance, it excels in tackling challenging off-road terrains with ease.
Both the petrol and diesel engines offer strong performance and ample torque, making highway cruising and off-roading effortless and engaging.
Compared to its predecessor, the new Thar offers significantly improved on-road driving dynamics, feeling more stable and manageable for daily commutes.
The ladder-frame chassis and overall construction give it a very robust and durable feel, implying longevity and resilience for rough use.
negatives: The off-road focused suspension and ladder-frame chassis result in a noticeably bouncy and unsettled ride, particularly for rear passengers on uneven roads.
As a two-door vehicle, access to the rear seats is cumbersome, and the boot space is severely limited, making it less ideal for family use or long trips.
While improved, the interior still features hard plastics and some ergonomic quirks, lacking the premium refinement expected from a modern urban SUV.
The steering can feel somewhat vague at higher speeds, and the high center of gravity means it exhibits noticeable body roll during enthusiastic cornering.
PowerDrift often highlights that the powerful engines combined with the vehicle's weight and aerodynamics result in less-than-stellar fuel efficiency, especially in city conditions.
